page_content:
Prerequisite: ECON602 and ECON604; or permission of BSOS-Economics department.

Recommended: ECON754.

ECON771 Advanced Labor Economics: Theory and Evidence (3 Credits) Provides students the theoretical background and econometric tools to engage and conduct research in labor economics. The course highlights the importance of a good balance between theory and solid empirical work. The topics include human capital, empirical earning functions, labor demand, incentive, discrimination, minimum wage, labor supply, tasks and skills, and inequality. In addition, it goes over essential topics in applied economics, including selection bias, instrumental variables, structural vs. reduced-form analysis, dynamic discrete choice models, and unobserved heterogeneity. During the term, students work with different data sets.
